questions.question: Explain the difference between preemptive and non-preemptive scheduling with examples.
expectedPoints: Preemptive scheduling allows interruption of running process
Non-preemptive scheduling runs process to completion or until it blocks

questions.question: Compare and contrast FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin scheduling algorithms, discussing their advantages, disadvantages, and suitable applications.
expectedPoints: FCFS: Simple but suffers from convoy effect
SJF: Optimal for waiting time but difficult to implement
Round Robin: Good for time-sharing but performance depends on quantum size
Applications: FCFS for batch systems, SJF for batch where burst times known, Round Robin for interactive systems
Starvation issues in SJF and Priority scheduling
